chapter_context.summary: Recounts Ezekiel eating the roll of the book, which was as sweet as honey in his mouth, his commissioning as a watchman to the house of Israel to warn both the righteous and the wicked, and his subsequent binding and silencing by God.

outline.text: Eating the Roll: Sweet as Honey in the Mouth
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Eating the Roll: Sweet as Honey in the Mouth (3:1-3), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Commissioned as Watchman to Tel-abib by the Spirit
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Commissioned as Watchman to Tel-abib by the Spirit (3:4-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: The Heavy Duties of the Watchman for the Wicked and Righteous
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Heavy Duties of the Watchman for the Wicked and Righteous (3:16-21), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

outline.text: Restricting and Silencing the Prophet on the Plain
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Restricting and Silencing the Prophet on the Plain (3:22-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Ezekiel.

key_themes.title: Prophecies Against Judah and Jerusalem
key_themes.description: This chapter forms a core part of Ezekiel's prophecies concerning prophecies against judah and jerusalem, illustrating God's absolute sovereignty in both justice and final restoration.

key_themes.title: The Glory of the Lord
key_themes.description: A central theological theme in Ezekiel, describing the overwhelming majesty of God's presence, its departure due to sin, and its return in future restoration.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the visions, symbolic actions, and warnings of Chapter 3, what does this teach you about God's holiness and His desire for deep, covenant integrity in your own life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to rely on the Spirit's empowerment to stand on your feet and witness faithfully in a challenging culture?

cultural_historical.title: The Exile in Babylon
cultural_historical.text: Ezekiel ministered in exile among the deportees by the River Chebar, providing a crucial priestly voice that helped preserve Israel's faith during the collapse of the monarchy and the destruction of the Temple.
